records.ety: [OE. norture, noriture, OF. norriture, norreture, F. nourriture, fr. L. nutritura a nursing, suckling. See Nourish.]

defs: The act of nourishing or nursing; tender care; education; training.
quotes: A man neither by nature nor by nurture wise.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Nurtured (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Nurturing.]
usage: -- To Nurture, Nourish, Cherish. Nourish denotes to supply with food, or cause to grow; as, to nourish a plant, to nourish rebellion. To nurture is to train up with a fostering care, like that of a mother; as, to nurture into strength; to nurture in sound principles. To cherish is to hold and treat as dear; as, to cherish hopes or affections.

defs: To educate; to bring or train up.
quotes: He was nurtured where he had been born.
